headline: Alphabet’s $17b Bond Sale Reshapes AI Spending And Valuation Story
summary: Alphabet, ticker NasdaqGS:GOOGL, has completed a multicurrency bond offering raising over $17b. The deal includes large euro and Canadian dollar tranches and is aimed at funding AI expansion and infrastructure. The new financing is linked to expected record AI infrastructure and data center spending in 2026. For you as an investor, this funding move sits at the heart of Alphabet's core story as a global technology platform focused on search, cloud, and AI. The decision to issue more than...

headline: Larry Miller, NYU Music Business Professor: Fortt Knox Innovation Lab
summary: At the Amplify Music Investment Summit, NYU Professor Larry Miller calls streaming the biggest of a century's music disruptions, with catalogs fetching $1.3 billion-plus after peer-to-peer nearly gutted IP. Spotify won on UX where a sued-out Napster could have. China is now the fourth-largest market via ad tiers. Taylor Swift's clockwork releases and $2 billion-plus Eras Tour contrast Jay-Z's portfolio play across media and culture; Bad Bunny's Spanish Super Bowl drove streaming.

headline: Thailand's SiamAI denies exporting US AI servers to China
